LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND GUARDSMARK, LLC DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION, DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY. PLAINTIFFS DO NOT OPPOSE THE DEMURRER, BUT REQUEST LEAVE TO AMEND. THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO STATE A CLAIM UPON WHICH RELIEF CAN BE GRANTED BECAUSE IT FAILS TO ALLEGE THAT A CONDITION OF THE PUBLIC PROPERTY CONTRIBUTED TO THE INJURY. (SEE PEAKER V. CITY OF SAN DIEGO (1994) 30 CAL.APP.4TH 909, 913-917. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/JMQ)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GUARDSMARK, LLC AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, DEFENDANTS MOVE TO STRIKE THE FOLLOWING ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT, WHICH RELATE TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES: PARAGRAPH 29, PAGE 5:11-12; PARAGRAPH 37, PAGE 6:15-16; PARAGRAPH 47, PAGE 7:20-21; PARAGRAPH 56, PAGE 8:10-11; PARAGRAPH 63, PAGE 9:1-2; PARAGRAPH 70, PAGE 9:19-21; PARAGRAPH 80, PAGE 10:25-26; AND PRAYER FOR RELIEF #3, PAGE 11:11. PLAINTIFFS DO NOT OPPOSE THE MOTION, BUT REQUEST LEAVE TO AMEND. THE ALLEGATIONS ARE IMPROPER BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO STATE A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. (SEE CIV. CODE, SEC. 3294(D); GOV. CODE, SEC. 818.) THE IDENTIFIED ALLEGATIONS ARE STRICKEN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS SHALL FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN TEN DAYS. DEFENDANTS SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/JMQ)

IF DEFENDANTS BELIEVE THAT THERE ARE ANY PLEADING DEFICIENCIES IN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT, THEY CAN FILE A DEMURRER AND/OR MOTION TO STRIKE. MS. POITRAS MUST FILE A CCP 377.32 DECLARATION WITHIN TWENTY DAYS. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR #6576, BUS. Off Calendar Jul-08-2016 Continued To Sep-07-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Strike And Or Dismiss Complaints
Off Calendar Jul-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Attorney, Conor Granahan, Esq. (415-830-3325) appears for plaintiff/non-opposing party, Kim Riportella

No other appearances are made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GUARDSMARK, LLC's Motion To Strike And Or Dismiss Complaints is GRANTED as to plaintiff Kim Riportella and DENIED as to plaintiff Louise Poitras. Ms. Riportella apparently no longer wishes to be a plaintiff in this action and her failure to file an amended complaint requires that she be dismissed as a plaintiff. Ms. Poitras' filing of a first amended complaint on May 16, 2016 shows that she does wish to be a plaintiff in this action and her one day late filing is not a basis to preclude her from doing so. If defendants believe that there are any pleading deficiencies in the amended complaint, they can file a demurrer and/or motion to strike. Ms. Poitras must file a CCP 377.32 declaration within twenty days. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-04-2016 Continued To May-25-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Matter heard at 2:00 p.m. No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Guardsmark, LLC demurrer to Plaintiffs' eighth cause of action, dangerous condition of public property. Plaintiffs do not oppose the demurrer, but request leave to amend. The eighth cause of action fails to state a claim upon which relief can be granted because it fails to allege that a condition of the public property contributed to the injury. (See Peaker v. City of San Diego (1994) 30 Cal.App.4th 909, 913-917. The demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. The prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Matter heard at 2:00 p.m. No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS GUARDSMARK, LLC AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, Defendants move to strike the following allegations of the complaint, which relate to punitive damages: Paragraph 29, page 5:11-12; paragraph 37, page 6:15-16; paragraph 47, page 7:20-21; paragraph 56, page 8:10-11; paragraph 63, page 9:1-2; paragraph 70, page 9:19-21; paragraph 80, page 10:25-26; and Prayer for Relief #3, page 11:11. Plaintiffs do not oppose the motion, but request leave to amend. The allegations are improper because the complaint fails to state a claim for punitive damages. (See Civ. Code, Sec. 3294(d); Gov. Code, Sec. 818.) The identified allegations are STRICKEN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiffs shall file an amended complaint within ten days. Defendants shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/JMQ)
